key_points: River Houses highly coveted; housing lottery creates stress
Dorms range from historic to brutalist concrete buildings
Some students essentially live in libraries during finals
key_quotes: 'Is my friend actually living in the library? For seven days my phone shows the same little pin in the same corner'
'That dude loved chilling in my wigg dorm'
'I scraped through Harvard with a B- average in the 80s'

key_points: Wealth gap between rich and scholarship students is stark and visible
Exclusive clubs exist but most students are too studious to party much
Social scene reportedly less intense than rumored; many friends too studious to party
key_quotes: 'I didn't feel particularly unsafe most of the time? If anything I just had friends who were too studious to even party that much'
'Two types of exceptional at Harvard: exceptional academic achievements or exceptional family wealth'
'I had a coworker who left us when he graduated from Harvard and got one of those consulting jobs, and it made him so miserable he came back after a year. We're freaking baristas'

key_points: Annenberg Hall is iconic and visually stunning
The Coop bookstore workers report being underpaid and overworked
Campus food quality not a major discussion point in student posts
key_quotes: 'Ever wondered what we, the underpaid, overworked, and non-harvard-affiliated employees feel like when you're bu...'
'Prospective students see way better food than they ever do on a normal day because a bunch of prospective students are here'
'The Coop was so bare bones... wild to see it turned into the library from Hogwarts'

key_points: Grade inflation cap proposal triggered crying, skipped classes, student outrage
Sleep deprivation is normalized; students brag about running out of paper studying
Students openly question whether they made the most of their experience post-graduation
key_quotes: 'The whole entire day, I was crying. I skipped classes on Monday and was just...' — student reacting to grading report
'Sleep deprived is exactly how I would describe my university experience'
'I graduated this year and have been struggling with feeling like I made the most of the college experience... I'm already quite disillusioned with the institution'

key_points: Need-blind aid is genuinely generous for families earning under ~$200k
Students above the aid threshold face brutal sticker price with no middle ground
International grad students face near-impossible funding situation with no co-signer options
key_quotes: 'I got in, can't afford it — I don't qualify for need-based aid, so the cost is largely on my family and me'
'Your parents are honestly quite selfish if they make enough that you don't qualify for fin aid (aka >>$200k/yr) and are unwilling to help'
'Harvard said no deferral just for finances' — international MPH admit

key_points: CS50 with Prof. Malan is legitimately world-class; students globally rave about it
Grade inflation is severe — over 50% of grades are As, faculty voting to cap at 20%
Asian international students frequently say Harvard is easier than expected
key_quotes: 'Currently in university in England and I have never seen a lecture at this quality and nor have I ever seen a lecturer so enthusiastic'
'Harvard admits all straight A students and then wonders why they all continue to get straight As'
'I thought it would be HARDER' — Indian student at Harvard

key_points: Ad Board dismissal process described as opaque, bureaucratic, and merciless
Harvard removed 800 grad students from union, claiming they aren't employees
Staff layoffs of 25% at SEAS and FAS triggered by funding uncertainty; workers devastated
key_quotes: 'The Ad Board bureaucracy, the no-mercy decision making...' — dismissed student
'Utterly depressed and dismayed over the ongoing union staff layoffs. We are losing the very fabric of what makes this university function'
'Complete and utter BS' — reaction to Harvard removing 800 students from grad union

key_points: Harvard Square and Cambridge are genuinely beautiful, especially in snow
Bike and scooter safety in and around the Yard is a real daily hazard
Campus is walkable but pedestrian infrastructure near Science Center is dangerous
key_quotes: 'These shots are so cinematic! Making me very homesick for Harvard'
'I always have to look both ways when I leave the yard bc I'm scared of being run over by a bike or a scooter'
'I used to walk from main campus to HBS daily and my goodness the sense of peace was almost overwhelming'

key_points: Harvard name alone doesn't guarantee better outcomes than peers nearby
Consulting and finance jobs exist but reported to cause misery in practice
The Harvard degree carries international recognition but local neighbors earn comparable salaries
key_quotes: 'I live right near Harvard, which means I know a lot of people that went there, they are all in the exact same positions making the same or less than the other adults I know'
'I had a coworker who left us when he graduated from Harvard and got one of those consulting jobs, and it made him so miserable he came back after a year'
'you think you are going to change the world, but actually you are there to keep the world EXACTLY as it is'

key_points: For families under ~$200k income, Harvard is often cheaper than state schools
Middle-income families earning just above aid thresholds face worst deal
Free online courses like CS50 offer genuine Harvard-quality education at zero cost
key_quotes: 'Fun fact: Harvard has free courses online that you can attend and when you finish, you get a certificate that you can then put on your CV'
'Got in, can't afford it — offered a full ride to another university, and from their perspective it doesn't make sense to turn that down'
'I 100% financed a HYPSM education and I have no regrets. I paid off my loans in a few years'

key_points: Students are driven, sleep-deprived, and self-aware about elite bubble they inhabit
Wealth inequality on campus is jarring and openly discussed by students
Harvard brand carries social baggage — graduates often hide it by saying 'school in Boston'
key_quotes: 'My brother went to Harvard. He said well I thought so too until I saw how many idiots they let in'
'It's called the H-bomb. Once you mention it in conversation, it derails the moment'
'Just say you went to school in Boston like the rest of us'

key_points: Epstein ties: Multiple senior faculty including Larry Summers sought Epstein's advice on pursuing women they mentored
Legacy/wealth access: 43% of white admits are ALDC; 75% of those would have been rejected otherwise
Labor practices: kitchen staff forced to commute in blizzards as 'essential workers'; Coop workers paid below Dunkin wages
key_quotes: 'when i was first looking at the course catalogue this summer and saw his name as a professor i was like ...oh he's still allowed to teach huh'
'More than 43% of white student admits at Harvard are ALDC admissions... Roughly three-quarters of white ALDC admits would have been rejected absent their ALDC status'
'Is Harvard forcing its kitchen staff to drive to work during the storm again? They declared them essential workers during the last storm and I'm still furious about it'

key_points: Sanders Theater philosophy lectures were historically open to the public — just walk in
Graduate seminars open to undergrads offer smaller, more rigorous intellectual experience
Remy the Harvard Cat is a beloved campus fixture who visits dorms and buildings
key_quotes: 'About 7 years ago, Professor Sandel was still teaching this course and the public could sit in on all the lectures — just walk into Sanders Theater and take a seat'
'Take grad seminars. Graduate students are cool, especially HDS kids'
'That dude loved chilling in my wigg dorm. Glad to see he's still around' — about Remy the cat
